member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute

member.param: The priority of the attributed member. Higher numbers are prioritized, lower numbers are deprioritized. 0 is the default if no attribute is present.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.

member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Index
member.summary: Represent a type can be used to index a collection either from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Construct an Index using a value and indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.param: The index value. it has to be zero or positive number.
Indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.remarks: If the Index constructed from the end, index value 1 means pointing at the last element and index value 0 means pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: P:System.Index.Start
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at first element.

member.name: P:System.Index.End
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromStart(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the start at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the start.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromEnd(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the end at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the end.

member.name: P:System.Index.Value
member.summary: Returns the index value.

member.name: P:System.Index.IsFromEnd
member.summary: Indicates whether the index is from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.GetOffset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the offset from the start using the giving collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the Index will be used with. length has to be a positive value
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter and the returned offset value against negative values. we don't validate either the returned offset is greater than the input length. It is expected Index will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. If the returned offset is negative and then used to index a collection will get out of range exception which will be same affect as the validation.

member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.EngineExtensionMethods.AdjustForPriorTarget(Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.VersionRange)
member.summary: Adjusts the version range for prior target compatibility by incrementing the maximum version's major version by 1 if bounded.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.AtomRepository.LoadFeedAsync
member.summary: This method actually does the work to download the feed. It should only ever run once per instance of this class. It runs on a background thread, so it is safe to make blocking calls (e.g. hitting the network).

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.AtomRepository.LoadOOBFeed
member.summary:
Load the legacy notification fragment.
3 keys here: a. If there is an update, the uri will ends with +_UpdateFeed. If there is no update, the uri will ends with -_UpdateFeed. b. Make sure the setupCommnadLine attribute has a value = update. This will ensure that we call willow with the update switch c. The version is supposed to be higher than installed version only when there is a mnaifest update
member.returns: Return the feed

member.summary: Determines if given extension is applicable to current SKU
member.param: extension to check applicability for
member.returns: true if extension is applicable or if the atom feed entry does not specify explicit list of applicable SKUs

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionEngine.Impl.Settings.SingleFileWatcher
member.summary: Wraps a FileSystemWatcher and does not inherit as we are explicitly looking at a single file for updates and need to guard against it not existing yet.. We need to only know about true changes to the file, so we must debounce. Debouncing is required because of the way windows events happen, a file save can have two: 1. Writing content. 2. Writing metadata. For some reason these two steps are not done in one step, and thus the caller must either handle double processing, or we must batch updates. This implementation chooses to batch updates in a configurable interval with a default of

members.member:
Dictionary used to track the lifetime of multiple asynchronous operations.
Change to Concurrent dictionary

member.summary: The main Install method that performs the bulk of the installation work. This includes checking for install dependencies or conflicts, writing the extension files to the install directory, and enabling the extension upon success. This method is also recursive in nature since it needs to install an extension's nested extensions. Asynchronous callers will pass an AsyncOperation object, in addition to two ulong values used to report install progress. Synchronous callers will just pass null for the AsyncOperation object to denote a synchronous invocation. The ulong install progress values are ignored in the synchronous case since we don't need to report install progress.
member.param: The extension to be installed.
A list of extensions that have been installed by this method. This list is only used to roll back the install in the case of an install failure or cancellation.
A list of extensions that have been Uninstalled by this method. This list is only used to roll back the install in the case of an install failure or cancellation.
A copy of the list of installed extensions that contains any changes made during one install transaction.
The Asynchronous Operation helper used to report progress during installation. If this method is called synchronously, this parameter should be null.
The total size in bytes that will be written during the installation of the parent extension.
The number of bytes written so far.
member.returns: True if a restart of Visual Studio is required for the extension to be loaded.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.ExtensionInstallModule.InstallSynchronously(Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.IInstallableExtension,Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.InstallFlags,System.Version)
member.summary: Calls the BeginInstall method with a null AsyncOperation object.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.ExtensionInstallModule.BeginInstall(Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.IInstallableExtension,Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.InstallFlags,System.ComponentModel.AsyncOperation,System.Version)
member.summary: This is the installation entry point for both synchronous and asynchronous installs. It will initialize the total install size for the asynchronous case as well as the list of extensions installed and uninstalled by this invocation. It then calls the public recursive installation method which will perform all installation tasks and install nested extensions recursively.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.ExtensionInstallModule.CheckIfExtensionRequiresAdditionalOperationForInstall(Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.IExtension,Microsoft.VisualStudio.ExtensionManager.Def.Settings.IInstalledExtensionList,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean@)
member.summary:
This method checks if the extension that we're trying to install should result in an additional operation (for example, upgrading or enabling an existing extension). Extensions which are not already installed, being installed as an experimental extension, or are being replaced require no additional operation.
If the extension is already installed and installed extension is per-machine, we allow per-user extension for the same extension. Same applies for reverse. If already installed extension in per-user, we allow per-machine extension for the same extension. If the extension is already installed and the installed version is different from the extension we're checking against, we throw an exception. If the two versions are the same, the behavior differs based on whether the new extension is a nested or a parent installation. In the case of a parent install, the method throws an exception. In the case of a nested install, the method succeeds.
member.returns: The method returns true if the extension installation may be skipped or upgraded and false if the installation must proceed. Blockers that are found will be thrown as Exception types.

member.summary: This method checks if the extension requires an operation in addition to an install (for example, an upgrade or an enable operation, or because this is a nested extension). Nested extensions are treated slightly differently than parent extensions in the case of an already installed extension. Throws an exception if the installation should be stopped.
member.param: The new extension we're installing.
A copy of the list of installed extensions that contains any changes made during one install transaction.
Denotes whether the new extension is a nested extension in another VSIX.
True if an older version of the same extension is already installed and the extension should be updated.
member.returns: The method returns true if the extension installation may be skipped or upgraded and false if the installation should proceed. Blockers that are found will be thrown as Exception types.

member.summary: This method compiles a list of all extensions that will be broken as a result of an upgrade performed by an extension.
member.param: The extension to check for possible upgrade problems.
[in, out] A list of extensions impacted extensions that will be appended to.

member.summary: This method compiles a list of all extensions that may be broken as a result of an upgrade performed by a nested extension. For example, assume extension A-1.0 depends on installed extension B-1.1. Also, extension C contains a nested copy of extension B-1.2. Upgrading extension B during the installation of C may break extension A.

An enumeration that states whether an extension is in an Installed or Uninstalled state. An extension may end up in an uninstalled state after installation if an error occurred and the installation was rolled back.
Denotes whether a restart is required to load the extension.
Contains any error that occurred during installation. This value is null if the install was successful.
Denotes whether an install was cancelled in the middle of installation.
The AsyncOperation object used to marshal the install completion event to the caller.

member.summary: Installs a parent extension's nested extensions. Extensions that are already installed will be skipped.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.VsAppIdProperty
member.summary: Int values used to specify what properties to get from IVsAppId. These should match their VSAPropId counterparts under IVsAppId

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.IVisualStudioCacheInvalidationHandler
member.summary: Interface used for handling extension cache, and the per-user/per-instance timestamps in Visual Studio.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.PerInstanceTimestampCacheInvalidationHandler
member.summary: Creates a per-instance specific timestamp invalidation handler that reads from the timestamp property.
member.remarks: Invalidating the per-instance timestamp is not supported.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.PerInstanceTimestampCacheInvalidationHandler.InvalidateAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.exception: This exception is always thrown because per-instance timestamps are read-only and cannot be manually invalidated.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.PerUserTimestampCacheInvalidationHandler
member.summary: Creates a per-user specific timestamp invalidation handler that reads and/or updates the timestamp property.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.PerUserTimestampCacheInvalidationHandler.InvalidateAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Calls into the to set the , providing the reason why a timestamp update is being performed.
member.param: Source of the invalidation, usually as "{Class}.{Method} {Action}".
Cancellation token for this asynchronous task.
member.returns: Task to track the asynchronous work.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.TimestampCacheInvalidationHandler
member.summary: Generic timestamp invalidation handler that can manage either a per-user or per-instance timestamp.

member.summary: Gets the latest per-user or per-instance configuration timestamp from VSAppID using a
member.param: Cancellation token for this task.
member.returns: UTC of the latest per-user or per-instance timestamp if running under devenv. Returns otherwise.
member.remarks: Uses to choose between per-user and per-instance locations

member.summary: Compares our extension cache timestamp against the latest per-user or per-instance configuration timestamp to determine if the cache is still valid.
member.returns: True if valid, false otherwise

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.TimestampCacheInvalidationHandler.GetAppIdTimestampPropertyAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Acquires an service to access IVsAppId properties and verifies the value is .
member.returns: representing the timestamp for the specified . Returns if IVsAppId service isn't available (e.g. VSIXInstaller).

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.TimestampCacheInvalidationHandler.GetTimestampWithOffsetString(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Provides a DateTimeOffset formatted as "yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffzzz", where fffzzz is timezone offset.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.VersionedCacheInvalidationHandler.IsCacheValidCoreAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.CachedExtensions{Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.CachedExtension},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Checks if the cache is valid after it has been loaded.
member.param: The loaded cache.
A token to signal cancellation.
member.returns: True if the cache is valid, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.VisualStudioCacheInvalidationHandler.GetCacheFileTimestamp
member.remarks: If this handler has been invalidated, this will return the minimum FILETIME value to force cache recreation. Otherwise, the timestamp will be retrieved from the underlying extension cache timestamp reader.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.ISdkExtensionLocator
member.remarks: Used by to check if SDK extension locators should run during the extension discovery process.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.MessagePackCacheUtilities`1.Microsoft#VisualStudio#Extension#Management#IStreamDeserializer{T}#ReadDataFromStream(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: This is intentional, we need to have the spans done in a sync method so the compiler can guarantee no context switching.

member.remarks: The version of native visible extensions aren't actually cached.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.PerUserEnabledListExtension.Version
member.remarks: The version of native visible extensions aren't actually cached.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.VisualStudioExtensionCache.cacheReadError
member.summary: Indicates whether or not there was an issue reading the current cache. Used during cache initialization to ensure all locators run in this scenario. This value is set to true when an exception is thrown during and is set back to false once the new cache is written successfully in .
Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.VisualStudioExtensionCache.SaveFullCacheCoreAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.CachedExtension},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Overrides the current extension cache with the provided extensions.
member.remarks: Only rebuilds the extension cache and doesn't update VS timestamps.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.VisualStudioExtensionCache.SaveCacheInternalAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{Microsoft.VisualStudio.Extension.Management.CachedExtension},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously saves the specified collection of cached extensions to the cache.
member.param: The collection of objects to be saved.
The reason for the cache update, used for telemetry.
A token to monitor for cancellation requests.
member.remarks: This method does not perform any operations if the host is not running within the expected environment. If is , the method will invalidate the configuration and extension cache before writing the extension data.
member.returns: Task tracking the asynchronous work.

member.remarks: These locators will only run if the cache was invalidated on load.
